prevalence.source: EAU guideline context and epidemiology syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant LUTS burden and QOL impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DRE, PSA, IPSS symptom score, urinalysis; uroflowmetry and ultrasound when indicated

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions of men with LUTS
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High; access to medications and minimally invasive therapies is broad

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EAU-aligned evaluation with broad access to diagnostics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Alpha-blockers, 5-ARIs, combination therapy; minimally invasive procedures and TURP for refractory cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Balancing PSA-driven evaluation with overdiagnosis concerns
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Comprehensive urology services and wide access to effective therapies
